title: Licaria limbosa
intro: Licaria limbosa, also known as the Cinnamomo, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Lauraceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Licaria limbosa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and other infections.
taxonomy: Licaria limbosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Alzateraceae. It belongs to the genus Licaria, which is a group of tropical and subtropical evergreen trees. The plant is native to Central and South America. Licaria limbosa is a member of the order Laurales, a group of flowering plants that are often found in humid, tropical environments. Licaria limbosa is also known as the White Licaria due to its white flowers.
